title: The Web's First Spark
story: A brilliant idea sparked in 1989 that would change the world forever. Computer scientist Tim Berners-Lee dreamed up a way to connect information across computers. On March 12, he handed his boss at CERN a proposal for an 'information management system.' Though the plan seemed a bit fuzzy, his boss found it 'vague, but exciting.' Little did they know, this was the seed that would grow into the World Wide Web!

title: Gandhi's Salty Protest March
story: Mahatma Gandhi laced up his sandals and set off on an incredible journey. In 1930, he began a 200-mile walk across India to protest an unfair salt tax. The British rulers had made it illegal for Indians to collect or sell salt without paying extra money. Gandhi's peaceful march inspired thousands to join him, showing the world the power of non-violent resistance. This bold action on March 12 helped spark India's fight for independence.

follow_up_question: Why do you think Gandhi chose to protest peacefully instead of fighting?

title: Double Trouble: The Twin Boom
story: Seeing double? You're not alone! In 2021, researchers announced we'd reached 'peak twin.' More twins were being born than ever before - about 1 in every 42 babies! That's a whopping 1.6 million twins arriving each year. Scientists think this twin trend might slow down, but for now, the world is full of dynamic duos.
activity_question: According to the story, how many babies are twins?

story: History was made in Bristol, England in 1994. The Church of England welcomed its first female priests on March 12. At Bristol Cathedral, 32 women were ordained as priests. This groundbreaking event opened new doors for women in the church. It marked a significant shift in centuries-old traditions.
activity_question: Where did this historic event take place?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it took so long for women to become priests in the Church of England?

title: A Musical Takes Flight on Broadway
story: Broadway welcomed a heartwarming new show in 2017. Come From Away, a musical by Irene Sankoff and David Hein, soared onto the stage at Schoenfeld Theatre on March 12. The play tells the true story of a small town in Newfoundland that opened its arms to stranded travelers after the 9/11 attacks. Its message of kindness touched audiences worldwide.
activity_question: What is the name of the musical that opened on Broadway in 2017?
follow_up_question: What real-life event inspired the story of Come From Away?

title: Trailblazing College Opens Doors for Women
story: Cheers erupted as Mississippi made history! On March 12, 1884, the state established America's first public college just for women. This groundbreaking school opened new paths for ladies seeking higher education. Before, many universities only accepted men. Now, women had a place to learn, grow, and chase their dreams.
activity_question: What kind of college did Mississippi establish?
activity_choices: America's first public college just for women
A school for both boys and girls
activity_answer: America's first public college just for women
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important to have a college just for women back then?

title: Batter Up! Yankees Join the Big Leagues
story: Baseball fever swept through New York City in 1903. The American League welcomed a new team: the New York Highlanders. Little did fans know, this scrappy squad would evolve into the legendary Yankees. On March 12, the league officially approved the franchise, setting the stage for countless thrilling games and a dynasty that would dominate America's pastime.
activity_question: What was the original name of the New York Yankees?
bonus_fact: The team's nickname 'Yankees' became official in 1913.
